title: About Performance Interpreting
subtitle: Built from the community up — by someone who understood the need first-hand.
metaDescription: Performance Interpreting was founded by Marie — who is hard of hearing herself — to ensure Deaf audiences have equal access to live events across the UK and Ireland.

sections.label: Our Story
sections.heading: Access that comes from lived experience
body: Performance Interpreting was founded by Marie, who is hard of hearing herself. That personal connection isn't a footnote — it's the foundation. PI didn't start because someone spotted a gap in the market. It started because Deaf audiences were being left out of some of the best live experiences in the country, and that was unacceptable.

From the very first event, PI has been shaped by genuine understanding — what it feels like to sit in a venue and not be able to follow what's happening on stage. That understanding drives every decision about how our interpreters are briefed, positioned, and supported on the day.

sections.label: Recognition
sections.heading: Industry recognition and legal milestones
body: Marie was inducted into Signature's Hall of Fame — the highest recognition in the BSL interpreting industry — in acknowledgement of outstanding contribution to interpreting services and Deaf access.

PI's work has shaped the legal landscape too. Our testimony in the landmark Little Mix BSL case helped establish that BSL access at live events is a legal requirement, not a courtesy. That ruling changed how the entire industry thinks about Deaf inclusion.

items.title: Signature Hall of Fame
items.description: Inducted for excellence and outstanding contribution to BSL interpreting services.

items.title: Little Mix BSL Legal Case
items.description: PI's testimony helped establish BSL access at live events as a legal right under UK equality law.

items.title: Royal Events & State Ceremonies
items.description: Selected to manage full Deaf access — BSL, captions, looping systems — for Royal events and state ceremonies.

sections.label: Accreditation
sections.heading: Professional standards, rigorously maintained
body: All Performance Interpreting interpreters hold NRCPD registration — the UK's national register for communication professionals working with Deaf and deafblind people. This is the professional standard that organisations like the NHS, courts, and government bodies require.

We don't just check registration at onboarding. We maintain active oversight of interpreter qualifications, ongoing professional development, and conduct — because your event, and your audiences, deserve that assurance.

sections.label: Our Scale
sections.heading: 500+ events. Every major stage.
body: Over more than a decade, Performance Interpreting has delivered BSL and ISL interpreting at over 500 events across the UK and Ireland. That includes 27+ major festivals, six Premier League clubs, and more than ten national arenas.

Our clients include Arsenal, The O2, Wembley Stadium, Royal Albert Hall, Live Nation, Festival Republic, BBC, UEFA, NFL, and many more. We have worked at Download, BST Hyde Park, Reading, Leeds, Creamfields, Latitude, and across the full Live Nation and Festival Republic portfolios.
